title: leontodon saxatilis subsp. hispidus
intro: Leontodon saxatilis subsp. hispidus, also known as Rough Hawk's Beard, is a subspecies of the lesser hawk's beard plant, a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and western Asia. Its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which contribute to its use in traditional medicine. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and relieving digestive issues, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Herbal preparations of Leontodon saxatilis subsp. hispidus typically involve drying and infusing the plant material in hot water or oil to create teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
